::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
```
:::


## Dataset

pokemon_df.csv


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pokemon_data <- readr::read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/refs/heads/main/data/2025/2025-04-01/pokemon_df.csv") 
```
:::


## Dataset Description

### Provenance

The data in this dataset came from [tidytuesday](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/tree/main/data/2025/2025-04-0), and was originally sourced and processed using [PokéAPI](https://pokeapi.co), an open-source, programmatic database containing comprehensive metadata about the Pokemon video game franchise. The dataset combines official in-game mechanical statistics (such as combat attributes and breeding groups) with multimedia design elements pulled directly from community-run encyclopedia archives like [Bulbagarden](https://bulbagarden.net).

### Dimensions and Structure

glimpse(pokemon_data)

:::
:::


### Why I Chose This Dataset

I chose this dataset because I have played every generation of Pokemon games since the original on Gameboy. I though it would be interesting to explore a dataset involving data from a game series that I am very familiar with as fan.

## Questions

The two questions we want to answer.

### Question 1

- How does a Pokemon's primary type and generation introduction interact to influence its physical durability compared to its special durability?

### Question 2

- Does a Pokemon's Body Mass Index (BMI) serve as a reliable predictor for its combat speed and physical attack power and follows the biological rule that heavier, bulkier creatures sacrifice speed for raw physical power?

## Analysis plan

### Question 1

Variables Involved:

- type_1 (primary elemental type)

- generation_id (generation introduced)

- hp (base hit points)

- defense (physical defense)

- special_defense

Variable to Be Created:

- Physical Durability = hp + defense

- Special Durability = hp + special_defense

Plan:

To answer the question we will combine the base stats of hp, defense, and special_defense into overall physical and special durability scores for each Pokemon. Then group the data by type and generation to create a multi-panel line graph that tracks how defensive playstyles evolved over the generations.

### Question 2

Variables Involved:

- weight

- height

- speed

- attack

Variable to Be Created:

- Body Mass Index (BMI) = weight / height

Plan:

To answer this question we will convert each Pokemon's height and weight into a single Body Mass Index (BMI) metric. Then create a scatter plot that uses point positions, sizes, and colors to show how a Pokemon's body size impacts both its speed and physical power.
